verses.text: 'O Israel, return unto the LORD thy God'; take words and pray, 'Take away all iniquity, and receive us graciously.' The way home. God even teaches the penitent how to plead.
'O Israel, return unto the LORD thy God'; take words and pray, 'Take away all iniquity, and receive us graciously.' The way home. God even teaches the penitent how to plead.

Asshur shall not save us, nor idols; 'in thee the fatherless findeth mercy.' God answers, 'I will heal their backsliding, I will love them freely.' Free grace. He loves the returning not for their worth but of His own mercy.

Asshur shall not save us, nor idols; 'in thee the fatherless findeth mercy.' God answers, 'I will heal their backsliding, I will love them freely.' Free grace. He loves the returning not for their worth but of His own mercy.
'I will be as the dew unto Israel: he shall grow as the lily,' revive as corn, and blossom as the vine. Refreshing grace. The healed soul flourishes under the gentle dew of God's favour.
'I will be as the dew unto Israel: he shall grow as the lily,' revive as corn, and blossom as the vine. Refreshing grace. The healed soul flourishes under the gentle dew of God's favour.
'I will be as the dew unto Israel: he shall grow as the lily,' revive as corn, and blossom as the vine. Refreshing grace. The healed soul flourishes under the gentle dew of God's favour.
Ephraim shall renounce idols, owning, 'from me is thy fruit found'; 'the ways of the LORD are right,' and the just walk in them. A wise close. The whole book ends with the path of life laid plain.
